name: The Things Network (TTN) LoRaWAN API
what_it_does: Community-operated public LoRaWAN network with REST and gRPC APIs — enabling IoT devices to connect to a globally distributed, crowd-sourced LoRaWAN network for free, with device registration, application server, join server, and network server APIs for device and data management, MQTT for real-time downlink/uplink, and a vibrant maker community.
use_cases: Registering LoRaWAN devices via TTN API for IoT device fleet management on public network
Receiving real-time device uplinks via TTN MQTT API for IoT sensor data streaming
Sending downlink commands to LoRaWAN devices via TTN API for remote device control
Managing TTN applications and integrations via API for IoT platform development
Querying TTN gateway coverage and network status via API for deployment planning
not_for: Private network deployments (use ChirpStack for self-hosted LoRaWAN)
Reliable SLA-backed IoT connectivity (TTN is community-operated — not guaranteed coverage)
High-bandwidth IoT (LoRaWAN is designed for very low data rates — not for streaming)
best_when: A maker, researcher, or early-stage IoT developer needs free LoRaWAN connectivity in an area covered by community gateways — for prototyping, urban IoT deployments, or connecting to the global maker community.
avoid_when: You need guaranteed coverage, SLA, or private network for production mission-critical deployments — use private ChirpStack or The Things Industries (enterprise) instead.

auth.notes: API key from TTN console. Bearer token auth for REST API. OAuth2 for web integrations. gRPC API with TLS. Scoped API keys per application/gateway. MQTT credentials (username: app-id, password: API key). Webhook for uplink data (HTTP integration). SDKs for JavaScript, Python, Go. TTN Community (free) vs The Things Industries (enterprise SLA).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community edition: free, best-effort connectivity via community gateways
pricing.paid_tiers: The Things Industries (enterprise): custom SLA pricing; Things Stack Cloud from $79/month
pricing.notes: Dutch non-profit foundation. Founded 2015 by Wienke Giezeman and Johan Stokking (Amsterdam). Community-run public LoRaWAN network. 150,000+ community gateways globally in 100+ countries. The Things Industries is the commercial arm (SLA, private networks). Open source: LoRaWAN Stack (Apache 2.0). The Things Conference (Amsterdam, yearly) is major IoT event. Used by: municipalities, universities, researchers, makers globally. Coverage: urban areas in Europe strongest, growing in Americas and Asia. No monthly fee for device connectivity on community network.

performance.rate_limits: Fair use: 30 downlinks/day/device; uplink: duty cycle limited by LoRaWAN spec

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + gRPC APIs. API key with scopes. MQTT for real-time data. SDKs for JS, Python, Go. Good documentation. Community coverage (not guaranteed SLA). LoRaWAN expertise required. GDPR compliant. 30 downlinks/day/device rate limit. Duty cycle limits from LoRaWAN spec (radio regulations).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device reads fully idempotent by DevEUI. Application reads idempotent. Downlink sends not idempotent — each send queues new downlink. Gateway reads idempotent by EUI.
known_agent_gotchas: Community network — NO guaranteed coverage or SLA; use The Things Industries for production SLA
30 downlinks/day/device limit — very restrictive for frequent remote control
LoRaWAN duty cycle limits (radio law) — cannot send uplinks continuously
Coverage varies by location — check community gateway map before deployment
gRPC primary + REST available — protocol choice affects client library selection

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. Scoped API keys per application. OAuth2 for web integrations. MQTT TLS for device data. LoRaWAN AES-128 encryption at protocol level. Open source (Apache 2.0) — auditable. Community foundation — non-profit governance. Device data routed through community infrastructure — evaluate for sensitive data. The Things Industries enterprise option for higher security requirements.
